summary: Iteration 27 - Pairwise-Derived Human Rankings feature testing. All 22 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI verified with both experiment sections rendering correctly - Pairwise-Derived (primary) and Average H1 Rating (secondary).

backend: GET /api/validation/pairwise-results returns 200
GET /api/validation/pairwise-results returns status=ok
Pairwise results has method=pairwise_derived
Pairwise results contains human_matches_derived (757 derived matches)
Pairwise results contains human_matches_ties_excluded (540 ties)
Pairwise results contains experts_contributing (21 experts)
Pairwise results contains pairwise_agreement object
Pairwise agreement has overlapping_pairs (221), agreements (124), agreement_rate (56.1%)
Pairwise results contains correlation metrics (spearman, kendall, pearson)
Pairwise correlation values in valid range [-1, 1]
Pairwise results contains comparison table
Pairwise comparison entries have all required fields (human_rank, human_score, human_win_rate, human_matches, ai_rank, ai_score, rank_delta)
Pairwise comparison entries have valid human values
Pairwise results contains interpretation text mentioning pairwise
Pairwise results contains expert_stats with papers_rated, pairs_derived, ties
GET /api/validation/results (avg-rating) still returns 200
GET /api/validation/results (avg-rating) returns status ok
Avg rating results contains correlation metrics
Avg rating results contains comparison with 47 papers
Both endpoints return ok simultaneously
Endpoints have different methods (pairwise vs avg)
Status endpoint still returns expected data (47 papers)
frontend: Validation page loads with 'Human vs AI Validation' title
H1 Papers stat card shows 47 papers
AI Matches stat card shows 500 matches (46.3% coverage)
Pairwise-Derived Human Rankings section visible as primary experiment
Pairwise Agreement gauge shows 56.1% with '50% = random' label
Stats line shows: 46 papers, 757 human pairs (540 ties excluded), 480 AI matches, 221 overlapping pairs
Pairwise table has Human Rank, H Score, H Win%, H Matches columns
Average H1 Rating section visible as secondary experiment
Avg rating table has H1 Rating column instead of H Score
Methodology mentions: Pairwise experiment (primary), Average rating experiment, AI tournament, Pairwise agreement, 50% = random

critical_code_review_comments: Pairwise-derived human ranking implementation correctly extracts head-to-head preferences from experts who rated multiple papers
Both human and AI rankings use Bradley-Terry algorithm - apples-to-apples comparison as intended
56.1% pairwise agreement (>50% random baseline) indicates AI slightly better than random at matching human preferences
Correlation between AI and human Bradley-Terry rankings is negligible (ρ=-0.046, p=0.76) - not statistically significant
Frontend correctly shows pairwise experiment as primary (first) and avg rating as secondary (below)
data-testid attributes properly implemented for all key UI elements
seed_data_creation: None - tested with existing validation data (47 papers, 500 AI matches, 21 contributing experts)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 27 verified the new pairwise-derived human ranking feature. The /api/validation/pairwise-results endpoint returns 757 human matches derived from 21 experts (540 ties excluded). Pairwise agreement with AI is 56.1% on 221 overlapping pairs. Both experiments (pairwise and avg-rating) coexist and work independently. The validation data includes 47 H1 papers and 500 AI matches.
